name: Persimmon Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill a large kettle with enough water to come halfway up the sides of a 2-quart pudding mold. Place the kettle over medium heat and let the water come to a boil while you prepare the pudding batter.
2. Ensure the mold has a lid or is snugly covered with foil during steaming (a coffee can with a plastic lid will work).
3. Place a rack or Mason jar ring on the bottom of the kettle so that water can circulate under the mold while it steams.
4. Grease the mold.
5. In a small bowl, combine persimmon puree and baking soda. Set aside while you mix the remaining ingredients (the persimmon mixture will become quite stiff).
6. In a mixing bowl, cream together butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
7. Add eggs, lemon juice, and rum to the butter mixture and beat well until smooth.
8. Gradually add flour, cinnamon, and salt to the egg mixture, stirring until just combined.
9. Stir in the persimmon mixture, nuts, and raisins until all ingredients are evenly distributed.
10. Pour the batter into the prepared mold and cover tightly.
11. Steam the pudding for 2 hours. Remove the mold from the kettle and let it stand for 5 minutes.
12. Turn the mold onto a rack to cool completely, or serve warm immediately.
